TORNADO RATED EF1 WITH WINDS OF 90 MPH. TRACK WAS FROM 1 MILE NORTH OF OLAR TO 2 MILES EAST-NORTHEAST OF EHRHARDT AND WIDTH WAS 300 YARDS. NUMEROUS TREES ARE DOWNED AND SEVERAL HOMES AND MOBILE HOMES WERE DAMAGED. STORM WAS ALSO ACCOMPANIED BY 2.75 INCH HAIL...BASEBALL SIZE. TREMENDOUS AMOUNT OF DAMAGE TO HOMES AND AUTOMOBILES, ALONG WITH WHEAT AND CROPS FLATTENED. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES REPORTED.

STORM SURVEY DETERMINED AN EF1 TORNADO, 86-110 MPH, TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTHWEST OF LONESTAR IN CALHOUN COUNTY, CONTINUED NORTHEAST ACROSS LAKE MARION INTO RURAL SUMTER COUNTY. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED IN THE LONESTAR AREA ALONG MCCORDS FERRY ROAD JUST SOUTH OF TINAH ROAD. AT THAT LOCATION SEVERAL HOMES LOST PORTIONS OF THEIR ROOFS AND NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES WERE DOWN. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST ACROSS BROOMSTRAW ROAD, SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS LOST ROOFS OR WERE DESTROYED.

POSSIBLE TORNADO. EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES, TREES AND POWER POLES DOWN AND DAMAGE TO AGRICULTURAL EQUIPMENT IN LONESTAR. DAMAGE REPORTED ON HWY 267, MCCORDS FERRY RD, BROOMSTRAW RD AND TYNAH RD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR AND EM REPORTS.
AIKEN COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED TREES DOWN AT THE BEECH ISLAND COMMUNITY.
TREES DOWN ON HWY 191 NEAR THE TOWN OF EUREKA.
TREES DOWN ON TRAILERS ON CHALK STREET.
